campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from the Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships in the city. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule (ordinance 2326) and moved forward with plans for a new bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 youth scholarship donation from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of interim ordinance 2326
New bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application submission for bridge planning and feasibility study

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council approved funding decisions for community grants, deciding which organizations would receive money and how much each would get. They also finalized rules for how groups can apply for money to host events and temporarily close streets in Saratoga.
keyTopics: Approved funding recipients and dollar amounts for secured grants
Set funding allocation for the Competitive Event Grant Program
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grant applications

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings through multiple channels including cable TV, online streams, and phone access. The meeting included translation services in over 50 languages to help non-English speakers follow along.
keyTopics: How to watch the meeting live (cable, YouTube, Zoom, or phone)
Free translation services available in 50+ languages
Meeting access options for residents without internet or cable
No public comment period noted for this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming agendas for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling for boards and commissions meetings
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for programs that help with housing and community development. The main focus was deciding which local organizations should receive federal grant money to support housing and services for residents in need.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant funding decisions for local nonprofits
Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) applications
Approval of previous meeting notes from February

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed through several new programs. They discussed a business license amnesty program to help shops get current with the city, launched a 'Shop Local' campaign for the summer, and explored ways to encourage new retail stores and startups to open in Cupertino through tax breaks and other incentives.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping shops catch up on licenses without penalties
Summer Shop Local Campaign - encouraging residents to buy from Cupertino businesses
City sponsorships with private businesses - funding events through partnerships
Economic incentives for retail and startups - tax breaks and programs to attract new businesses
Outreach to shopping centers - directly engaging landlords and merchants

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a Governance and Ethics Committee meeting on March 30, 2026. The city is continuing to offer both in-person and remote attendance options so residents can participate however works best for them.
keyTopics: In-person and remote meeting options available (Zoom and phone)
Public comment submission process (email by 10 a.m. the day before)
Governance and Ethics Committee activities
Multiple ways to participate: Zoom, phone, or in-person attendance

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to recognize Women's History Month and approve two key agreements. The council authorized a disaster relief partnership with the American Red Cross to use city facilities when emergencies occur, and approved a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software (Mimecast) costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
